sourceEvidence.label: Tibial Shaft Fracture — Fast study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Fast study template for Tibial Shaft Fracture

mustKnow: Soft tissue exam, compartments, alignment, weight-bearing plan
payload.oneLiner: Tibial shaft fractures are soft-tissue injuries first and bony injuries second.
pimpQuestions: What are compartment syndrome warning signs?
Why do open tibias need urgency?
orSurvivalTips: Always mention soft tissues and compartment checks
Know common IM nail starting point language

sourceEvidence.label: Tibial Shaft Fracture — Learning objectives

objectives: Describe initial stabilization and soft-tissue priorities in tibial shaft fractures.
Recognize the association with compartment syndrome.
Compare nonoperative treatment with IM nailing indications.

extractedSignals.summary: 10 high-confidence Anki card mapping(s) for curriculum node trauma-tibial-shaft-fractures
63 Orthobullets question mapping(s) for trauma-tibial-shaft-fractures (metadata only — no stems/answers)

metadata.description: Intramedullary nailing for unstable or operative tibial shaft fracture patterns.

metadata.claim_text: Tibial shaft fractures are soft-tissue injuries first and bony injuries second.
Segmental or highly comminuted tibial shaft patterns often signal instability and higher operative likelihood.

metadata.action: Proceed with operative stabilization pathway (typically IM nail when soft tissues allow) with documented compartment exam
metadata.trigger: Unstable alignment, open injury, segmental/comminuted pattern, or unacceptable deformity

metadata.claim_text: Proximal tibial entry point landmarks and nail starting point language are core OR survival knowledge for tibial IM nailing.
Present wound status, compartment check, alignment, splint plan, and weight-bearing restrictions with explicit compartment surveillance.

metadata.description: Angular or rotational malalignment after tibial shaft fracture.
Failure of fracture healing after tibial shaft injury.
Deep infection risk especially with open tibial shaft fractures.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Segmental Tibial Fracture Pattern

metadata.description: Segmented diaphyseal pattern suggesting instability and operative tendency.

existingProposals.evidence_summary: Pilot entity: Tibia Leg Anatomy Hub

metadata.description: Composite leg model linking tibial shaft, fibula, blood supply, and compartment layout.
Endosteal and periosteal perfusion concepts relevant to healing and open fractures.

metadata.claim_text: Treating an open tibial shaft fracture like a routine closed injury without soft-tissue urgency and antibiotic planning.

metadata.action: Nonoperative immobilization with serial compartment exams and repeat radiographs
metadata.trigger: Closed injury with acceptable alignment in patient suitable for casting/bracing

metadata.claim_text: Serial compartment exams matter because compartment syndrome is a clinical diagnosis that can evolve after splinting.
Fibula status can influence tibial alignment and length goals during fracture management.
